_source: Standard Byzantine Typikon (Antiochian Service Book / OCA / Jordanville Horologion). The weekday prokeimena are fixed by day of week and are tone-independent of the Octoechos weekly cycle. They are unchanged on ordinary days; feast prokeimena (appointed by Menaion or Triodion) override these on their respective days.
_weekdayIndex: Entries keyed by JS Date.getDay() value: 0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, … 6 = Saturday.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Sunday, Tone 6
prokeimena.text: The Lord is King; He is robed in majesty.
prokeimena.verse: The Lord is robed; He is girded with strength.
prokeimena.note: Sung at Sunday Vespers. The Lord's Day prokeimenon is invariable.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Monday, Tone 4
prokeimena.text: The Lord will hear me when I cry to Him.
prokeimena.verse: When I called, the God of my righteousness answered me.
prokeimena.note: Sung at Monday Vespers on ordinary days.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Tuesday, Tone 1
prokeimena.text: Thy mercy, O Lord, shall pursue me all the days of my life.
prokeimena.verse: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
prokeimena.note: Sung at Tuesday Vespers on ordinary days.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Wednesday, Tone 5
prokeimena.text: O God, in Thy name save me, and in Thy might vindicate me.
prokeimena.verse: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
prokeimena.note: Sung at Wednesday Vespers on ordinary days.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Thursday, Tone 6
prokeimena.text: My help comes from the Lord, Who made heaven and earth.
prokeimena.verse: I lift up mine eyes unto the hills; from whence cometh my help?
prokeimena.note: Sung at Thursday Vespers on ordinary days.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Friday, Tone 6
prokeimena.text: God is our refuge and strength, a helper found in troubles.
prokeimena.verse: Therefore we will not fear, though the earth be moved.
prokeimena.note: Sung at Friday Vespers on ordinary days.

prokeimena.label: Prokeimenon of Saturday, Tone 8
prokeimena.text: The Lord is King forever and ever; the nations shall perish from His land.
prokeimena.verse: Hear my prayer, O Lord; give ear to my supplication.
prokeimena.note: Sung at Saturday Vespers on ordinary days. Main verse from Ps 9 LXX (= Ps 10 Hebrew); verse from Ps 142 LXX (= Ps 143 Hebrew).
